skill.name: creating-bauplan-pipelines
skill.description: Creates Bauplan data pipeline projects with SQL and Python models, including project structure, DAG planning, materialization choices, and validation checks.
skill.summary: Create Bauplan pipeline projects with safe branching, SQL entry models, Python transformations, and data quality expectations.
skill.author: BauplanLabs

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer high-risk weak-crypto, Ruby backtick, dynamic import, and reconnaissance hits are false positives caused by Markdown examples, inline code, and normal Python imports. The skill still has medium operational risk because it can write project files, fetch Bauplan documentation, and run Bauplan CLI commands that affect remote branch and pipeline state.

medium_findings.title: External Bauplan CLI Operations
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for operational risk. The skill allows Bauplan CLI commands and directs users through branch creation, table inspection, dry runs, and pipeline runs. This is legitimate for a Bauplan pipeline skill, but it can change remote Bauplan branch or pipeline state if used without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool and workflow checklist directly show Bauplan CLI use. The commands are product-specific and bounded, so the concern is operational impact rather than malicious execution.

medium_findings.title: Workspace File Generation Capability
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for filesystem risk. The skill has Write access and instructs creation of project folders, configuration, SQL models, Python models, and expectation files. This is expected behavior, but generated files should stay within the intended project workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The front matter grants Write and the project structure section lists files to create. The capability is explicit and legitimate for the stated purpose.

low_findings.title: Restricted Documentation Network Access
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for limited network use. The skill permits WebFetch only for docs.bauplanlabs.com and links official Bauplan SDK documentation. No evidence found of credential exfiltration or arbitrary external endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network destination is visible and restricted to Bauplan documentation. This is still network access, but the context supports benign documentation lookup.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Markdown Backtick Execution Alerts
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The Ruby or shell backtick alerts are Markdown inline code, fenced examples, command names, and decorators. No evidence found of Ruby code execution or shell interpolation inside an executable script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation text with inline backticks. The skill files are Markdown instructions, not Ruby source using backtick execution.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Alerts
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The cited weak-cryptography locations contain descriptive text, headings, or a Markdown table, not calls to MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic APIs. No evidence found of cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review shows ordinary prose and table content. The static match appears to be substring-based rather than semantic cryptography detection.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Dynamic Import Alert
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The flagged section is a fenced Python example importing named Bauplan expectation helpers. It is not a JavaScript dynamic import expression and is not executed by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code uses a normal Python from-import statement in documentation. There is no computed module path or runtime dynamic import behavior.

low_findings.title: Dismissed System Reconnaissance Alerts
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The Bauplan commands gather account, branch, and table metadata needed for the workflow, and the example lines are data quality assertions. No evidence found of OS reconnaissance such as host, process, or network enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed commands are scoped to Bauplan project state and table schema. The examples.md locations are expectation code, not reconnaissance commands.

content.value_statement: Building a Bauplan pipeline requires branch safety, project structure, model dependencies, and validation rules. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through creating SQL and Python models with safe workflow checks.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of a Bauplan project folder and bauplan_project.yml file.
Plans pipeline DAGs with SQL first-node models and Python downstream models.
Defines materialization choices for intermediate and final output tables.
Uses Bauplan CLI workflow steps for branch safety, dry runs, and pipeline runs.
Shows I/O pushdown patterns with selected columns and filters.
Provides examples for data quality expectations and output column validation.
limitations: Requires the user to provide pipeline goals, source tables, and desired outputs.
Does not provide Bauplan credentials or create external accounts.
Cannot guarantee correctness until dry runs and user review pass.
References examples and documentation, not every Bauplan SDK feature.

use_cases.title: Start a New Analytics Pipeline
use_cases.description: Create a structured Bauplan project with source tables, transformations, materialized outputs, and validation checks.

use_cases.title: Standardize Pipeline Patterns
use_cases.description: Apply consistent SQL and Python model rules across a team, including branch safety and schema declarations.

use_cases.title: Add Data Quality Gates
use_cases.description: Create expectation files that check nulls, uniqueness, accepted values, and metric thresholds before publishing outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup with known source and output tables.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Bauplan pipeline for this goal: [goal]. Use source tables [tables] and produce output tables [outputs]. Ask me for any missing details before writing files.

prompt_templates.title: Plan the Pipeline DAG
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Bauplan DAG for [business logic]. Identify SQL first nodes, Python transformations, final materialized outputs, and required source table checks.

prompt_templates.title: Add Validation and Expectations
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve an existing pipeline with schema and quality checks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Bauplan pipeline and add output column declarations, docstrings, and data quality expectations for [quality rules]. Keep intermediate tables unmaterialized unless needed.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Strict Production Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced pipeline implementation with strict validation and branch controls.
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement a Bauplan pipeline for [domain] using strict mode. Create a development branch workflow, use I/O pushdown, define materialization strategy, and include dry-run verification steps.

output_examples.input: Create a daily trip summary pipeline from taxi trips and taxi zones.
output_examples.output: A project plan with source table verification, a SQL staging model, Python join and aggregation models, final materialized outputs, and dry-run steps.

output_examples.input: Add checks to ensure order IDs are unique and revenue is positive.
output_examples.output: A data quality plan with expectation names, target tables, checked columns, failure messages, and where each check fits in the pipeline.

output_examples.input: Convert an existing transformation into a Bauplan project.
output_examples.output: A migration outline that separates first-node SQL, downstream Python models, output schemas, materialization choices, and validation commands.

best_practices: Always create and use a development branch before running a Bauplan pipeline.
Use SQL only for first nodes that read directly from lakehouse source tables.
Declare output columns and use I/O pushdown whenever source schemas are known.
anti_patterns: Do not run pipelines on the main branch.
Do not materialize intermediate tables unless downstream users need them.
Do not read full source tables when a smaller column set or filter is sufficient.

faq.question: What information should I provide before using this skill?
faq.answer: Provide the pipeline goal, source tables, final output tables, and any materialization or strict mode preferences.

faq.question: Can it create both SQL and Python models?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses SQL for first nodes that read source tables and Python for downstream transformations.

faq.question: Does it run Bauplan commands automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide Bauplan CLI commands when allowed, but users should review branch and run commands before execution.

faq.question: Does it support data quality expectations?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns for expectation files and common checks such as nulls, uniqueness, and thresholds.

faq.question: Why does it warn about the main branch?
faq.answer: Running pipelines on the main branch can affect shared production state. The skill instructs users to work on development branches.

faq.question: Can it replace Bauplan documentation?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance and examples, but the official documentation remains the source for full SDK details.
